LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED ON DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF KENNETH ALLEN WHITE, II'S COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH STATUTORY MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 435.5(A) PROVIDES THAT BEFORE FILING A MOTION TO STRIKE, THE MOVING PARTY "SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE PLEADING THAT IS SUBJECT TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE WITH THE PURPOSE OF ATTEMPTING TO "RESOLVE[] THE OBJECTIONS TO BE RAISED IN THE MOTION TO STRIKE." THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. BOTH PARTIES ARE REMINDED THAT LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) REQUIRES THAT COURTESY COPIES OF ANY PAPERS FILED WITH THE COURT MUST BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED ON DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF KENNETH ALLEN WHITE, II'S COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH STATUTORY MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41 REQUIRES THAT THE DEMURRING PARTY, PRIOR TO FILING THE DEMURRER, "SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE COMPLAINT WITH THE PURPOSE OF ATTEMPTING TO "RESOLVE THE OBJECTIONS TO BE RAISED IN THE DEMURRER." THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. BOTH PARTIES ARE REMINDED THAT LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) REQUIRES THAT COURTESY COPIES OF ANY PAPERS FILED WITH THE COURT MUST BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted, as follows:

Defendant Smule, Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled. A claim for fraud may lie where a misrepresentation induces a plaintiff to enter into a contract, including an employment contract. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 638.) In Lazar, the court upheld recovery, under a claim for fraud, for damages incurred in relocating in reliance on misrepresentations by an employer. (Id. at 649.) However, a claim under Labor Code 970 is a distinct and separate cause of action. (See id. at 637 [plaintiff "set forth causes of action for: (1) violation of Labor Code section 970 . . . [and] (3) fraud?"].)

Defendant's argument that Plaintiff does not allege that Plaintiff reasonably relied on Defendant's representations lacks merit. (See FAC 14 ["Defendant's representations during the pre-hiring process led Plaintiff to reasonably conclude that his job position was long term . . . [which] induced Plaintiff to . . . move his family from Seattle, Washington to the San Francisco Bay Area."].) So does Defendant's argument that Plaintiff does not allege that Defendant knew the representations were false. (See FAC 16 ["The representations by Defendant to Plaintiff of the long-term nature of his job position were false and known to Defendant to be false. Defendant did not intend to abide by its statements..."].)

Defendant's argument that Plaintiff does not allege that Mr. Shang had the requisite authority to bind Defendant is overruled. The authority Defendant relies upon requires such pleading when asserting a fraud claim. (Hamilton v. Greenwhich Investors XXVI (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1602, 1614 (citing Lazar, 12 Cal.4th at 645).) Defendant does not show that there is such a requirement to plead a claim under Lab. Code 970, which applies to any "person, or agent or officer thereof . . . ."

The court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Defendant Smule, Inc.'s motion to strike punitive damages is granted. Plaintiff's first amended complaint seeks to state a cause of action for violation of Lab. Code 970. Lab. Code 972 provides that "any person, or agent or officer thereof who violates any provision of Section 970 is liable to the party aggrieved, in a civil action, for double damages resulting from such misrepresentations." Thus, the Legislature determined that double damages is the appropriate civil penalty for violations of section 970; it did not also authorize an award of punitive damages. Because the Labor Code expressly provides for the award of statutory penalties, "the Legislature's provision of such statutory penalties precludes an award of punitive damages." (In re Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. Wage and Hour Litigation (N.D. Cal. 2007) 505 F.Supp.2d 609, 620 [striking prayer for punitive damages]; see also De Anza Santa Cruz Mobile Estates Homeowners Assn. v. De Anza Santa Cruz Mobile Estates (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 890, 915 ["We have found no persuasive authority for the proposition that a plaintiff is allowed to recover punitive damages based solely on a statutory violation where the statute expressly provides for a penalty that is punitive in nature."].)

The court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'s Motion To Strike.

Defendant Smule, Inc.'s motion to strike Plaintiff Kenneth Allen White, II's complaint is off calendar for failure to comply with statutory meet and confer requirements. Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a) provides that before filing a motion to strike, the moving party "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to the motion to strike with the purpose of attempting to "resolve[] the objections to be raised in the motion to strike."

The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until September 9, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Both parties are reminded that Local Rule 2.7(B) requires that courtesy copies of any papers filed with the court must be delivered to Department 302.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Smule, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiff Kenneth Allen White, II's complaint is off calendar for failure to comply with statutory meet and confer requirements. Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 requires that the demurring party, prior to filing the demurrer, "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the complaint with the purpose of attempting to "resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer."

The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until September 9, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Both parties are reminded that Local Rule 2.7(B) requires that courtesy copies of any papers filed with the court must be delivered to Department 302.
